title: Workload widget should have option to display teams as individual members
excerpt: I have a workload widget set up to look at 3 boards. 1 of the boards assigns Teams to items while the others assign individual Members (people). Currently, the workload widget shows all of the people (from the 2 boards) …

posts.author: mpmcqueen (Matt McQueen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a workload widget set up to look at 3 boards. 1 of the boards assigns Teams to items while the others assign individual Members (people). Currently, the workload widget shows all of the people (from the 2 boards) and, separately, the Teams (from the 1 board). I would like for the widget to break the Team down into the individual team members and combine the workloads with the respective member.

In the example below, Araminta and Mark are part of the UK team and Will and Matt are part of the USA team. I would like the UK and USA teams’ workload to be applied to the members of each team.

image736×502 51.2 KB

posts.author: MHaigh (Matthew Haigh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, the workload is good, but really needs a few more features to make it AWESOME…
… the filters help you select the data that is used to populate the circles/workload… but after that we need a way to select/filter the PEOPLE that are shown…
MONDAY DEV TEAM: Easy win… please add a extra filter/multi select option so that when viewing the workload, you can choose to show one or more of the people listed, if possible a select TEAM option, that then only shows people who are members of that team. These filter what is shown (not what data is used to populate the workload)…
(this means we can have Teams to represent offices, or skills, and then show the workload of our specific teams)… yes, I know you can select 1 person at the moment, but with 600+ in the company, and 40 in a team, selecting one at a time is REALLY painful…

posts.author: mru (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: That is such a great point Matthew! Same issue we’re seeing. Can we please get some visibility on this issue? I think a lot of people could benefit from this being implemented.

posts.author: marcbrown (Marc Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: Please make this happen. Same issue with us!
